title: kairoa suberosa
intro: Kairoa suberosa, also known as the 'hairy box bush', is a small shrub native to Australia, characterized by its woody stems and leaves that have a velvety texture. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities. The health benefits of Kairoa suberosa include its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce swelling, and aid in wound healing. Herbal preparations of the plant often involve infusions, decoctions, or tinctures that can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
